author: kilrkrow
description: Look up and display Bible verses directly in your notes using the HelloAO Bible API. Supports inline references, code blocks, comparison views, and multiple Bible websites. No API key required. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

releases.author: kilrkrow
releases.description:
## What's Changed
* feat: make "Search selected text" work anywhere + document commands by @kilrkrow in https://github.com/kilrkrow/obsidian-bible-verse/pull/38
* fix: bug batch — baked nl flags, autocomplete braces, escaping, Source mode by @kilrkrow in https://github.com/kilrkrow/obsidian-bible-verse/pull/39
* fix: autocomplete brace/merge, \{} escaping, Source mode, numbered-book cursor by @kilrkrow in https://github.com/kilrkrow/obsidian-bible-verse/pull/40


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/kilrkrow/obsidian-bible-verse/compare/1.6.7...1.7.0

releases.author: kilrkrow
releases.description:
## What's Changed
* chore: address Obsidian plugin-review lint findings by @kilrkrow in https://github.com/kilrkrow/obsidian-bible-verse/pull/35


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/kilrkrow/obsidian-bible-verse/compare/1.6.6...1.6.7

releases.author: kilrkrow
releases.description:
## New

- **Bake to a native, collapsible callout** — the `native-callout` style (token `native-callout` / `nco`) writes a verse permanently into your note as a real Obsidian callout: `> [!quote]+ [John 3:16 (KJV)](…)`. It inherits your theme, icon packs, and native fold behavior. Use `nco-` to start it collapsed, `nco+` for expanded. The callout type (default `quote`) is configurable in settings.
- **`bake` token** — `{ref, bake}` bakes a reference into a ```bible code block on the spot.

Baking is one-way: once written, the text is ordinary Markdown and the plugin no longer tracks it. In the editor a "will bake" pill is shown; the bake happens when the note renders in Reading view.

**Installation:** copy `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` into `.obsidian/plugins/bible-verse/`.

releases.author: kilrkrow
releases.description:
## What's Changed
* feat: add ESV inline text via user-supplied API key by @kilrkrow in https://github.com/kilrkrow/obsidian-bible-verse/pull/26
* feat: in-plugin "Send feedback" button + report-bug command by @kilrkrow in https://github.com/kilrkrow/obsidian-bible-verse/pull/25
* feat: add GitHub issue forms (bug report + feature request) by @kilrkrow in https://github.com/kilrkrow/obsidian-bible-verse/pull/24


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/kilrkrow/obsidian-bible-verse/compare/1.6.3...1.6.4

releases.author: kilrkrow
releases.description:
## What's changed ### Fixed - **Verse double-spacing in Live Preview** — Verses in \ l\ mode rendered with a blank line between each verse in Live Preview. Root cause: CodeMirror 6's editor inherits \white-space: pre-wrap\ into rendered content, causing whitespace text nodes after each \
\ to render as extra line breaks. Fixed by setting \white-space: normal\ on the verse body container. - **Inline style line breaks** — \{ref, inline}\ displayed verses on separate lines instead of as continuous quoted text, in both reading mode and Live Preview. Fixed by replacing \
\ elements with spaces in the inline rendering path. Full details in [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/kilrkrow/obsidian-bible-verse/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md).

releases.author: kilrkrow
releases.description:
## 1.6.2 — Obsidian submission lint fixes

- Replace `builtin-modules` dev dependency with native `node:module`
- Replace `display:contents` on container span with `display:inline`
- Remove `!important` from paragraph margin rules; use scoped selectors instead

releases.author: kilrkrow
releases.description:
## What's new in 1.6.1

### New features
- **Reading sections** (para / 
o-para modifier) — groups verses by natural paragraph breaks from the source text
- **IntelliSense helper mode** — when a reference is complete, shows a menu of all available modifiers (style, formatting, reading sections)
- **Syntax reference in settings** — collapsible quick-reference table and inline code examples on each modifier setting

### Bug fixes
- Fixed cache key bug where 
o-nl / 
l overrides were ignored (verses were cached under wrong key)
- Fixed KJV pilcrow (¶) appearing as literal character or breaking verse/number layout
- Fixed verse numbers rendering as markdown ordered lists
- Fixed inline style paragraph stripping for multi-paragraph passages
- Fixed ault.modify → ault.process and ault.read → ault.cachedRead per Obsidian guidelines
- Fixed 
ormalizePath usage in cache module
- Updated settings tab to use setHeading() and sentence-case labels per Obsidian review guidelines

### Known issues
- [#21](https://github.com/kilrkrow/obsidian-bible-verse/issues/21) — 
l verse spacing and para paragraph spacing are visually inconsistent between reading mode and Live Preview; fix in progress for 1.6.2
